primary_catalyst.description: Monday open gap-down driven by weekend US/Israeli strikes on Iran (WTI >$78, flight to energy); NVDA and tech closed sharply off lows as intraday dip-buying returned following initial sector rotation into oil/defensives. Semiconductor weakness on the day framed by Iran-driven inflation/rates repricing, but NVDA specifically recovered on AI demand resilience narrative.

secondary_catalysts.description: Technology sector broadly under pressure as lingering AI-spending/monetization concerns pressured semis at the open

force_attributions.rationale: US-Iran conflict shock drove cross-asset risk-off open (oil +, rates +, growth -); this is the dominant regime event that produced the failed gap-down setup
Dip-buying flows and short-covering drove NVDA specifically from deep gap-down to +2.99% close — positioning, not fresh fundamental news
Hyperscaler AI capex narrative continued to underwrite buyers stepping in on NVDA-specific weakness

notes: Failed-gap pattern: geopolitical shock drove gap-down open, AI demand narrative drove intraday reversal to green close. Classic cross-current day.

primary_catalyst.description: Broad risk-off Friday: WTI crude broke above $90/bbl (week's 35% gain — biggest weekly oil move since futures trading began in 1983) as Iran war concerns kept Strait of Hormuz risk premium elevated. S&P -1.33%, Nasdaq -1.59%. Unexpected soft February NFP print (release morning of March 6) compounded inflation-plus-stagflation anxiety. NVDA dragged with Nasdaq in broader AI complex selloff.

secondary_catalysts.description: BlackRock capped withdrawals from private credit fund (first time) — amplified risk-off tone with credit-stress echo
Marvell +11% on AI-driven Q4 results — rotational pressure as diversification trade pulls flows away from NVDA even within AI theme

force_attributions.rationale: Oil shock + Iran risk + stagflation tape = cross-asset risk regime deterioration was the dominant driver on the day
Rotation into Marvell and other AI-adjacent names pulling flows out of NVDA (AI-diversification trade pattern visible all Q1)
NFP surprise + oil shock reduced likelihood of near-term Fed cuts; rate repricing pressured long-duration tech

notes: Confluence day: Iran-oil-rates-NFP all landed same session. Not a single-catalyst event.

primary_catalyst.description: Triple-witching Friday tech selloff. Super Micro (SMCI) crashed >20% premarket after Co-Founder Wally Liaw was charged with selling billions of dollars of servers containing sanctioned NVIDIA hardware to China in violation of export controls. Direct NVDA-supply-chain sanctions headline. Nasdaq -2% to 21,647.61, S&P -1.51%. AI-associated plays hit hardest. Russell 2000 entered correction territory.

secondary_catalysts.description: Triple-witching options expiration amplified positioning/gamma unwinds on heavy tech names
Post-FOMC (Mar 17-18) rate-cut uncertainty; Fed left rates unchanged citing Middle East/inflation risk

force_attributions.rationale: SMCI/Liaw sanctions-violation indictment put NVDA China export-control story front and center — direct hit on NVDA hardware supply chain integrity
Triple-witching expiration mechanically forced large unwinds in NVDA; heavy volume (+0.82 vol z) consistent with gamma/positioning-driven move
Ongoing Iran tension / broader risk-off tape provided the backdrop
FOMC held rates on Mar 18 citing Iran-inflation risk; tech duration discount pressure

notes: High-volume selloff (+0.82 vol z) on triple-witching expiration plus specific NVDA-linked sanctions headline — classic confounded day but C1 is the clearest NVDA-specific driver.

primary_catalyst.description: Modest down day amid re-escalation of US/Iran conflict headlines — Iran began charging Strait of Hormuz transit fees (up to $2M per voyage) and peace-process talk wavered. Mag7 continued slide (down 12-13% YTD at this point). Low-volume (-0.99 vol z) range-bound session; no NVDA-specific catalyst identified.

force_attributions.rationale: Iran geopolitical risk continued to weigh on Mag7 / growth complex; energy-transit-fee headline kept oil-risk premium alive
Low-volume drift within ongoing Mag7 de-rating trend; no fresh NVDA fundamental news

notes: LOW priority (range anomaly only, -0.25% close). Day-of news thin; treat as continuation of March Mag7 de-rating tape.

primary_catalyst.description: Bounce day within the broader March correction. No singular NVDA-specific catalyst identified; day fits technical-bounce pattern off prior-week extreme oversold levels as Iran/peace-talk headlines turned marginally constructive. GTC (Mar 16-19) product/backlog announcements from prior week continued to underpin bull case.

secondary_catalysts.description: GTC 2026 aftermath: $1T Blackwell/Rubin backlog through 2027 still being absorbed by analysts

force_attributions.rationale: Technical bounce / short-cover within a heavily oversold Mag7 complex; low volume (-0.67 vol z) consistent with flow-driven bounce, not fundamental catalyst
GTC $1T backlog narrative (Blackwell + Rubin demand from hyperscalers) continued to support dip-buying interest

notes: MEDIUM priority — no specific day-of catalyst found; most likely a positioning/technical bounce before Mar 26 plunge to YTD low.

primary_catalyst.description: Capitulation selloff to 2026 YTD low on confluence of: ARK Invest executed ~$84M in tech/semi divestments (sold 154,441-155,441 NVDA shares, ~$26-28M); US/Iran conflict re-escalation reignited risk-off; 'higher-for-longer' rate fear plus China-export-control drag compounded. Classic institutional distribution day into a broken tape.

secondary_catalysts.description: NVDA traded below S&P 500 forward P/E for first time in years amid Mag7 12-14% YTD decline
Iran-linked oil risk still elevated; SMCI sanctions scandal fresh (six sessions prior)

force_attributions.rationale: Institutional distribution (ARK dump, Mag7 de-rating, concentration unwind) was the dominant driver — positioning-flow event
Iran-war risk regime remained the backdrop pushing capital out of long-duration growth
China export controls (H20, SMCI sanctions echo) continued to drag NVDA-specific narrative
Higher-for-longer rate fear compressed tech multiples into quarter-end

notes: YTD low. Capitulation print set up the Mar 31 Marvell-deal reversal that followed. Moderate confidence on attribution weights — confluence day.

primary_catalyst.description: NVIDIA announced $2 billion strategic investment in Marvell Technology and deep strategic partnership using NVLink Fusion to co-develop next-generation AI infrastructure, including custom XPUs, networking solutions, and silicon photonics collaboration. Marvell +13%, chip complex rallied. Quarter-end reversal off YTD low. Volume z +2.02 confirms institutional re-engagement.

secondary_catalysts.description: Quarter-end window dressing / Mag7 mean-reversion buying off YTD low
NVIDIA-Marvell NVLink Fusion press release from NVIDIA newsroom

force_attributions.rationale: $2B Marvell stake signals NVDA confidence in hyperscaler custom-silicon AI infrastructure demand through 2027+ — core demand-cycle story
NVLink Fusion + silicon photonics extends NVDA rack-scale platform moat at the packaging/networking layer — a supply-side advanced-packaging claim
Co-opting Marvell's custom-silicon capability into NVIDIA ecosystem directly blunts the Trainium/MTIA/Maia displacement threat — defensive against D2 competitive pressure
Quarter-end flows amplified the move; high volume (z +2.02) consistent with institutional re-engagement off YTD-low capitulation print

f1_attribution.rationale: Marvell is a Tier 4 adjacent AI supplier (custom silicon, networking, photonics) and the $2B NVDA stake is independent 3rd-party corroboration of NVDA's view of AI demand — high-signal validation event

notes: CRITICAL single-catalyst day. +5.59% / +3.17 close z / +2.02 vol z — the Marvell deal is the clear singular driver. Reversal off the YTD-low capitulation print made 3/26.

primary_catalyst.description: Modest continuation day post Marvell-deal relief rally. No singular NVDA catalyst identified. Broader semiconductor complex grinding higher as AMD-Meta $60B deal (announced late Feb) continued to drive 'AI diversification' trade — NVDA lagged but participated in broad tape strength.

force_attributions.rationale: Low-volume (-1.51 vol z) drift continuation of Mar 31 reversal; positioning/flows dominate
Ongoing $1T Blackwell/Rubin backlog narrative underpinning buy-the-dip interest

notes: LOW priority — gap filled / follow-through day post Marvell rally. No discrete catalyst.

primary_catalyst.description: Flat session on exceptionally low volume (-2.69 vol z). No NVDA-specific catalyst identified. Market in wait-and-see mode ahead of expected Iran-war-related headlines and the subsequent April 7 ceasefire announcement. Options positioning (P/C shift from 0.53 to 0.78) showed growing caution ahead of May earnings.

force_attributions.rationale: Range-bound drift on near-record-low volume — pure positioning/flows session, no fundamental catalyst

notes: LOW priority — volume anomaly only. Lowest-volume session in the cohort; typical pre-event quiet day.

primary_catalyst.description: MAJOR overnight gap +3.59% on President Trump's two-week US/Iran ceasefire announcement (April 7 evening). Reversed weeks of Iran-war-driven tech selloff. NVDA/Meta/Amazon/Alphabet led Mag7 recovery. Chip complex surged: TSM +6%, ASML +9%, AMAT +9%, MU +8%, WDC +9%, LRCX +10%, INTC +11%. Trump administration also withdrew draft worldwide AI chip licensing proposal that would have required US approval for every large-scale GPU export — direct NVDA C1 positive.

secondary_catalysts.description: Trump administration withdrew draft worldwide AI chip licensing proposal — removed overhang on NVDA international revenue
Nasdaq/S&P 500 booked best week since November following ceasefire; Strait of Hormuz supply-chain relief for TSMC/NVDA

force_attributions.rationale: Iran ceasefire was the dominant cross-asset risk regime shift — oil plunged, USD softened, risk-on resumed. Dominant driver of the +3.59% gap
Withdrawal of AI chip licensing proposal directly removed a key NVDA export-controls overhang — specific NVDA-positive policy event
Strait of Hormuz / TSMC supply chain relief — reduced tail-risk to NVDA foundry supply
Short-covering and rotation flows back into Mag7 after months of de-rating

notes: HIGH priority — major overnight gap. Geopolitical regime shift the dominant force; AI chip license withdrawal is NVDA-specific kicker.

primary_catalyst.description: Follow-through day after April 8 Iran-ceasefire gap. NVDA closed at $182.08. Wedbush analyst note: NVIDIA 'undervalued given future AI monetization'. Continued risk-on relief rally; no new discrete catalyst. Low volume (-1.67 vol z) consistent with drift continuation.

force_attributions.rationale: Low-volume follow-through; positioning continuation from Apr 8 ceasefire gap
Continued benefit from ceasefire-driven risk-on tape

notes: LOW priority. Drift day — no discrete catalyst, continuation of ceasefire relief.

primary_catalyst.description: TSMC released March 2026 monthly revenue report: NT$415.19B, +45.2% YoY and +30.7% MoM — record March print. Q1 2026 revenue NT$1.134T (+35.1% YoY). Direct read-through to NVDA (NVDA is TSMC's largest advanced-node customer and books CoWoS capacity through 2027). TSM Taipei shares +2.3%; translated into NVDA bid through the session.

secondary_catalysts.description: TSMC record revenue framed as AI-chip-demand-stays-strong per CNBC coverage

force_attributions.rationale: TSMC monthly revenue print is the strongest leading indicator of NVDA demand fulfillment — advanced packaging + foundry signal
45% TSMC March YoY confirms hyperscaler AI capex is not just holding but accelerating — direct A1 validation
Continued post-ceasefire recovery flows supportive

f1_attribution.rationale: TSMC is Tier 4 adjacent AI supplier — independent 3rd-party corroboration that AI chip demand from NVDA hyperscaler customers is intact/accelerating

notes: MEDIUM priority but cleanly attributable to TSMC monthly revenue. Beginning of the 10-day winning streak.

primary_catalyst.description: NVIDIA launched 'Ising' — the world's first open AI models for quantum computing — on World Quantum Day (4.14 = first three digits of Planck constant). Ising Calibration (VLM) + Ising Decoding (3D CNN): up to 2.5x faster and 3x more accurate quantum error correction vs traditional. Adopted by Harvard, Lawrence Berkeley, Fermi Lab, Academia Sinica, IQM, Infleqtion, NPL. Quantum stocks surged: IONQ +20.95%, QBTS +22.63%, RGTI +13.28%. This was the 10th consecutive NVDA up-day — longest winning streak since 2023.

secondary_catalysts.description: 10-day winning streak momentum: Vera Rubin demand narrative, Q1 FY27 $78B guidance, 10-day MA crossed bullish above 50-day MA (technical breakout)
TSMC Q1 earnings pending (delivered Apr 16 with +58% profit surge, but rally anticipated it)
Cadence expanded AI alliances with TSMC, NVIDIA, Google on agentic AI / advanced-node design flows (April 2026 announcement)

force_attributions.rationale: Vera Rubin hyperscaler demand + $78B Q1 FY27 guidance narrative was the core fundamental driver of the 10-day streak that culminated Apr 14
Ising launch expands NVDA TAM into enterprise/research quantum-AI workloads — Ising Calibration + Decoding positions NVDA as quantum OS layer
Technical breakout above multi-week resistance (10/50 MA cross) drove CTA/trend-following flows; momentum chase after weeks of consolidation
Pre-positioning into TSMC Apr 16 earnings (which delivered +58% profit surge two days later)
Iran ceasefire-era risk-on regime continued supportive despite Apr 14 'bad wholesale inflation print' — market shrugged it off

notes: CRITICAL — this is the wedge breakout the user missed during vacation. Ising is the specific April 14 headline but the move is confounded with 10-day streak dynamics (TSMC, Rubin backlog, technical breakout). Primary attribution is A1 (demand cycle narrative) since the 18% 10-day move was fundamentally about Rubin/hyperscaler capex, with Ising as the headline-grabbing accelerant.

primary_catalyst.description: Continuation of 10-day+ winning streak following TSMC Apr 16 earnings blowout (+58% profit, $35.6B revenue, 61% HPC mix, 'AI demand exceptionally strong'). Low volume (-0.12 vol z). Drift higher; no discrete NVDA catalyst day-of, but TSMC earnings the prior day continued to feed the rally.

secondary_catalysts.description: CNN: oil drops, stocks soar to wrap up a wild week post-ceasefire

force_attributions.rationale: TSMC Q1 earnings (prior day) — 58% profit surge, AI demand 'exceptionally strong', $39-40.2B Q2 guide — continued to carry NVDA
Hyperscaler demand narrative continuing to compound as AI capex visibility extends to 2027
Momentum/trend flows riding the multi-day streak

f1_attribution.rationale: TSMC earnings is Tier 4 corroboration — NVDA's primary foundry confirming AI demand is accelerating

notes: LOW priority day but cleanly attributable to TSMC earnings read-through (prior day) + streak momentum.

primary_catalyst.description: Profit-taking / streak-break session. Stocks dropped on concerns that US-Iran peace deal would not be struck before ceasefire expiration Wed Apr 22. S&P -0.63% to 7,064.01, Nasdaq -0.59%. AMD +~3% (diversification trade still in play), QCOM -1%. Google (April 22) announced two new in-house AI chips (Ironwood TPU v7) — custom silicon displacement narrative weighed on NVDA.

secondary_catalysts.description: Google unveiled 2 new AI chips (TPU Ironwood) positioned as NVDA competitor — custom silicon displacement headline (article dated Apr 22 but news flow likely ran into Apr 21)

force_attributions.rationale: Low-volume (-1.66 vol z) profit-taking after 10+ day streak; positioning unwind, not fundamental reversal
Google TPU v7 / Ironwood announcement re-engaged the custom silicon displacement narrative
Iran ceasefire expiration anxiety re-entered the tape ahead of Apr 22 deadline

notes: LOW priority. Profit-taking after big streak; TPU announcement from Google the fundamental kicker on the downside.

primary_catalyst.description: Tesla Q1 2026 results weighed on AI/growth complex: TSLA -3.59% to $373.60, lifted 2026 capex guide to $25B (+$5B) for AI/robotics and indicated negative FCF for rest of 2026. IBM -8%, ServiceNow -~18% on disappointing prints — broad software/tech sell-the-news day. S&P -0.42%, Nasdaq -0.89%. NVDA dragged with the group ahead of Intel's after-close earnings (which would drive the +4.32% Apr 24 move).

secondary_catalysts.description: IBM, ServiceNow Q1 misses dragged enterprise software; Lockheed -5%

force_attributions.rationale: Sell-the-news tape across enterprise software (IBM, NOW) dragged broader tech; NVDA as largest tech component got hit with the basket
IBM/NOW weak enterprise AI prints called enterprise-AI-adoption narrative into question temporarily
Iran ceasefire uncertainty still lurking in backdrop
TSLA capex raise to $25B could be read bullish A1 (more AI capex) but market read as 'negative FCF = growth-at-any-cost' — net negative for AI complex on the day

notes: MEDIUM priority. No NVDA-specific news — dragged by broader tech weakness. The setup for the Apr 24 Intel-driven +4.32% reversal.

primary_catalyst.description: Intel Q1 FY26 earnings (released after close April 23) affirmed hyperscaler CPU demand: $13.6B revenue (vs $12.7B YoY), strongest top-line growth since 2021. DCAI segment +22% YoY to $5.1B. CEO cited three drivers: (1) accelerated Xeon 6 ramp at hyperscalers preparing 2027 capacity, (2) stronger-than-expected commercial PC refresh (Win10 EOL), (3) incremental growth. Revenue beat midpoint of guidance by $1.4B. Validates AI data center buildout as real, not vaporware. Intel +24% (best day since 1987). NVDA closed at $208.27 (+4.32%), first record close since October, market cap >$5T. Semiconductors rose for 18th straight day.

secondary_catalysts.description: CNBC: Nvidia stock closes at record, pushing market cap past $5 trillion — 4.3% jump on Intel print
Hyperscaler capex projected ~$650B across MSFT/AMZN/GOOGL/META/ORCL for 2026 per INTC DCAI context

force_attributions.rationale: Intel DCAI +22% YoY to $5.1B is the cleanest independent Tier 4 validation of hyperscaler AI capex being real and accelerating — Xeon 6 hyperscaler ramp for 2027 capacity IS the NVDA-adjacent CPU portion of AI rack spend. This is the dominant A1 force on the day.
AI data center buildout validation also indirectly validates power/grid infrastructure demand — CPU ramp implies rack-level buildout is executing
Breakout-day momentum + semis rising 18 straight days drove CTA/trend chase; record-close FOMO amplified the move
Intel's strength is marginally a competitive story (CPU TCO gaining vs pure-GPU rack design) but on this day the AI-validation signal dominated the competitive-threat reading

f1_attribution.rationale: Intel Q1 FY26 is the textbook Tier 4 F1 event: adjacent AI supplier (CPU side of rack) independently corroborating that hyperscaler capex is not only intact but accelerating. This is independent of NVDA itself and directly confirms the A1 demand-side thesis.

notes: CRITICAL — TODAY. Single-catalyst day: Intel Q1 FY26 earnings released after close Apr 23 drove gap-up Apr 24 open. F1 Tier 4 multiplier (1.5x) applied to A1 attribution. First NVDA record close since October; market cap >$5T.
